title: Zanthoxylum lenticellosum

intro: Zanthoxylum lenticellosum, also known as Japanese prickly ash, is a type of flowering plant in the Rutaceae family, native to Japan and Korea. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues such as pain, inflammation, and gastrointestinal discomfort, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Zanthoxylum lenticellosum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Zanthoxylum lenticellosum is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
